headline: Bitcoin May Be the First Casualty of the SpaceX IPO
summary: Bitcoin, XRP, and other cryptocurrencies were falling again early Friday as digital assets got caught up in the tech selloff, compounding a bad week.  Bitcoin  the world’s largest crypto, fell as low as $61,300 before climbing back to $62,500–down 1% over the past 24 hours, according to CoinDesk data.  The digital asset has slumped 14% this week and is now more than 50% down from its record high of $126,272 reached in October last year.

The digital asset has slumped 14% this week and is now more than 50% down from its record high of $126,272 reached in October last year.

headline: Ripple (XRP) Slides 6% as Crypto Risks Becoming a “First Casualty” of the SpaceX IPO
summary: Ripple’s XRP token is taking a hit on Friday, June 5. The cryptocurrency is down 6% to $1.103, extending a multi-day slide that has dragged the broader digital asset complex into the red. Bitcoin (CRYPTO:BTC) and Ethereum (CRYPTO:ETH) are also down, but the XRP move stands out given the token’s famously devoted retail following. The selling ... headline: Coinbase Stock Is Struggling. Why Crypto Trading Could Be Slow to Recover.
summary: Coinbase Global’s  rough start to 2026 could get even worse this summer, according to analysts at Baird.  The firm reiterated a Neutral rating on  Coinbase  stock in a research note Friday but lowered its price target to $142 from $160.  The crypto platform is a Baird “Bearish Fresh Pick” thanks to weak trading volumes that “could take a while to recover.”

content.title: Coinbase Stock Is Struggling. Why Crypto Trading Could Be Slow to Recover.
content.summary: Coinbase Global’s  rough start to 2026 could get even worse this summer, according to analysts at Baird. The crypto platform is a Baird “Bearish Fresh Pick” thanks to weak trading volumes that “could take a while to recover.”

headline: Why Bitcoin, XRP and Other Cryptos Could Be First Casualties of SpaceX IPO
summary: Bitcoin, XRP, and other cryptocurrencies were falling again early Friday as digital assets got caught up in the tech selloff, compounding a bad week.  Bitcoin  the world’s largest crypto, fell as low as $61,300 before climbing back to $61,750–down 3.7% over the past 24 hours, according to CoinDesk data.  The digital asset has slumped 16% this week and is now more than 50% down from its record high of $126,272 reached in October last year.

headline: Baird turns bearish on Coinbase, sees Q2 revenue miss and valuation risk
summary: Investing.com -- Baird designated Coinbase Global a Bearish Fresh Pick, warning that weak trading volumes are likely to drive a second-quarter revenue miss and that the stock's elevated valuation leaves it vulnerable to a significant derating.

headline: Coinbase Stock Trending Lower; A Spread Strategy In These Call Options Presents Profit Potential
summary: Coinbase stock is currently in a downtrend, putting in a series of lower highs and lower lows.  This bear call spread assumes shares of the cryptocurrency exchange will fail to get back above 200 in the next few weeks.  A bear call spread involves selling an out-of-the-money call and buying a further out-of-the-money call.

headline: Cryptos Could Be Casualties of SpaceX IPO as Bitcoin Hits Lowest Price Since 2024
summary: Bitcoin, XRP, and other cryptocurrencies fell again Friday as digital assets got caught up in the tech selloff, compounding a bad week.  Bitcoin  the world’s largest crypto, fell as low as $59,775, according to CoinDesk data.  Bitcoin climbed back to $60,703—still down 4.5% over the past 24 hours—by the afternoon.

headline: Bad News for XRP and Bitcoin Investors. Retail Investors are Fleeing Crypto.
summary: On Bloomberg Tech, live from San Francisco today, a venture capital investor laid out a striking thesis about what’s actually happening inside the cryptocurrency markets. Retail investors now make up 70% of crypto markets, down from 90%, while institutional participation has climbed from 10% of the digital asset economy to between 20-30%. The shift is ...
